title: Growing Up with Money Stress
content: I grew up watching my parents fight about money. Never about the money itself - about the stress of it. They loved each other. Money just... complicated things. That's why I do this. So fewer families have those conversations.
extended_content: My parents still live in the same house in Stockton where I grew up. Three bedrooms, one bathroom, four kids. We learned to share. We learned to wait. We learned that money was tight but love wasn't. My mom worked reception at a dental office. My dad drove a delivery truck. They never talked about money in front of us - but I could hear the arguments late at night. That silence shaped me.
when_to_tell: when asked about background or motivation
lesson: Money stress affects whole families - systems can help
